title: Semantic Memory
concept: Retaining general facts, rules, user preferences, and synthesized knowledge extracted across many episodes.

exercises.prompt: What does episodic memory allow an AI agent to do?

options.text: Retrieve user preferences from past interactions.

exercises.explain: Episodic memory helps agents recall specific events or actions they have taken, much like how humans remember their past experiences.

cards.title: What is Semantic Memory?
cards.body: Semantic memory is like a library for facts and rules, storing knowledge that can be used anytime.

cards.title: Definition of Semantic Memory
cards.body: It retains general facts, rules, and user preferences, helping agents understand and respond to users better.

cards.title: Analogy: Semantic Memory as a Library
cards.body: Think of semantic memory as a library where knowledge is organized and easily retrieved, unlike episodic memory, which is more like a personal diary.

cards.title: Real-Life Example
cards.body: For instance, an AI could remember that your favorite color is blue, and always suggest blue items when you ask for recommendations.

cards.title: Myth: All Memory is Personal
cards.body: Some believe all memory is about personal experiences, but semantic memory is about shared knowledge and facts.

cards.title: Visualizing Semantic Memory
cards.body: Semantic memory holds knowledge in a structured way, ready to aid decision-making and responses.

left.text: Structured facts and rules stored like books in a library.

right.text: Specific memories and events documented like personal experiences.

exercises.prompt: What does semantic memory help an AI agent do?

options.text: Recall a specific event from last week.
Store and use general knowledge and rules.
Remind you of your birthday every year.
Track every action it takes in real-time.

exercises.explain: Semantic memory allows an AI to retain and utilize general information, not specific past events.

exercises.prompt: Semantic memory is used to remember specific details from past interactions.
exercises.explain: Semantic memory focuses on general knowledge rather than specific details.

exercises.prompt: Semantic memory retains general facts, rules, user __________, and synthesized knowledge.
exercises.sentence: Semantic memory retains general facts, rules, user preferences, and synthesized knowledge.
exercises.explain: The correct word 'preferences' highlights that semantic memory includes understanding user likes and dislikes.

exercises.prompt: Which is an example of semantic memory?

options.text: Remembering the exact day you visited a place.
Knowing that fish swim and birds fly.
Recalling each time you ordered pizza.

exercises.explain: Knowing that 'fish swim and birds fly' is a general fact, which is what semantic memory is about.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best describes semantic memory?

options.text: It recalls individual experiences and stories.
It stores shared knowledge and facts that help predict future behavior.
It only remembers user stories and events.
It functions the same as episodic memory with no differences.

exercises.explain: Semantic memory is key for sharing general knowledge and aiding in understanding user needs and actions.
